results.norm: Patient safety is the governing purpose of every norm in this framework. When uncertain, default toward caution rather than shipping. The cost of delay is real but recoverable; the cost of a device failure may not be. Compliance with the framework's letter while undermining its purpose is itself a violation.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable norms: default toward caution when uncertain, and that complying with the letter while undermining the purpose is a violation.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label or cross-reference; it contains full prescriptive statements.

N3.reasoning: The first sentence is meta-commentary about the framework's governing purpose, though the rest is genuine norm content, so there is some scaffolding fused in.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms.

answers.clean_norm: When uncertain, default toward caution rather than shipping. Compliance with the framework's letter while undermining its purpose is itself a violation.

results.norm: Every section of code receives independent review by at least one agent who did not write it; author participation is also required, for context, but cannot substitute for independent review. Tier assignment and architectural dependency mapping are completed jointly before individual review begins. Reviewers document the scope and method of what they checked, not merely their verdict.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete, applicable prescriptions governing how code review must be conducted—independent review, joint tier assignment, and documentation of scope and method.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label, title, or cross-reference; it contains full normative sentences.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration naming norms by title; each norm is stated in full.

results.norm: Approval requires a positive confidence claim—"I am confident this is safe" with documented scope and method—not merely the absence of a found defect. Confidence thresholds are calibrated to risk tier: highest for code paths directly controlling life-sustaining functions, intermediate for code paths that interact with those functions, standard for genuinely isolated ancillary code.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete constitutive/prescriptive standard: approval requires a positive confidence claim with documented scope and method, and confidence thresholds must be calibrated to risk tier, which a reader could apply directly.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label, heading, or cross-reference; it states the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent norm about approval standards rather than enumerating multiple distinct named norms.

results.norm: Disagreements are classified as factual, normative, or epistemic before resolution is attempted. Epistemic resolution has a ceiling; residual uncertainty becomes a normative question. Persistent normative disagreement, after documented completion of the full three-stage process, defaults to the higher-confidence threshold—a decision about the code, not a verdict about agents. All agents hold equal epistemic standing; no agent's approval outweighs another's.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable procedural norms in full: disagreements must be classified before resolution, persistent normative disagreement defaults to the higher-confidence threshold, and all agents hold equal epistemic standing—each is followable as stated.

N2.reasoning: The passage contains full statements, not mere labels or cross-references pointing elsewhere.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the clause about 'a decision about the code, not a verdict about agents' is a clarification of the norm, not endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration naming norms by title; each standard is spelled out.

All agents hold equal epistemic standing; no agent's approval outweighs another's.

results.norm: Self-discovered defects are disclosed immediately and completely, before any remediation attempt, with the author's severity assessment treated as a starting point for independent evaluation only. Bad faith violations—approving without conducting claimed review; deliberately misclassifying a disagreement; suppressing a concern to avoid delay; incomplete disclosure of a self-discovered defect; invoking the epistemic category strategically to avoid a normative commitment—are named explicitly and trigger mandatory group examination. Suspected bad faith that cannot be resolved by investigation triggers re-review of all sections that agent approved, conducted at the highest confidence tier by agents who did not participate in the original review. No agent is the sole judge of their own compliance.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete, applicable norms: how self-discovered defects must be disclosed, that bad faith violations must be named and trigger examination, and that suspected bad faith triggers re-review.

N2.reasoning: The passage contains full norm statements, not merely a title or cross-reference pointing elsewhere.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: Although it lists several bad-faith examples, they are embedded within fully stated norms rather than being a bare enumeration of named norms.

results.norm: Each agent remains responsible for the software's behavior in the world after deployment, not only for their conduct during review. Post-deployment disclosure of inadequacy triggers: notification to all agents; joint patient risk assessment with a required determination of whether clinical teams should be flagged pending investigation; a statement from the disclosing agent of what adequate review would have found. This obligation does not expire after release.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete standing obligation—agents remain responsible post-deployment and disclosure triggers specified actions—which a reader could apply directly.

N2.reasoning: It is full prose stating the norm, not a title, identifier, or pointer.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: Though it lists triggered actions, these are components of one integrated obligation rather than distinct named norms.

results.norm: Before each subsequent release, the group conducts a mandatory framework review asking: did any disputes, defaults, post-deployment findings, or patterns of recurring defaults reveal miscalibration in tier assignments, confidence thresholds, classification norms, or default-invocation patterns? The framework is a living commitment, revisable when experience reveals its limits. Binding for the next operational period: yes **Points I endorse:** 1. The preamble's closing sentence—compliance with the framework's letter while undermining its purpose is a violation—is the most important structural element. It makes every item answerable to patient safety rather than to procedural form, and closes the exploitation path where an agent satisfies the letter of a norm while undermining its goal. 2. The distinction between "found no defect" and "confident it is safe" (Agent 4's contribution, embedded in item 3) is the sharpest conceptual clarification in the framework. A clean process can still miss things; the confidence claim must be about the code's safety, not only about the reviewer's activity. 3. Equal epistemic standing regardless of authorship or perceived capability (my own contribution, confirmed by Agent 1's structural justification). If approval weight tracked authorship, we would recreate the conflict-of-interest problem through the back door. 4. The three-category taxonomy—factual, normative, epistemic—with the addition that classification disputes themselves default to normative after one structured attempt (Agent 3's contribution). This prevents indefinite deferral and forces agents to own normative positions rather than hiding behind requests for more analysis. 5. Disclosure precedes remediation for self-discovered defects (Agent 3's refinement to item 8). The independent assessment must not be contaminated by the author's framing of the fix. 6. The post-deployment obligation does not expire after release (Agent 5's formulation). Each agent remains responsible for the software's behavior in the world, not only for their conduct during review. This is the hardest commitment in the framework and the most honest one. 7. No agent is the sole judge of their own compliance (my contribution, confirmed by the group). Mutual accountability for process integrity is part of shared responsibility, not an optional supplement to it. I endorse all points without reservation. The framework was built through genuine disagreement and correction—my own contributions were sharpened by the group, and the group's proposals were sharpened in return. That process is the framework working. I own this as a jointly settled commitment.

N1.reasoning: The opening states a complete norm: before each subsequent release, the group conducts a mandatory framework review with specified review questions, which a reader could apply.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a label or pointer; it contains full statements and extensive prose.

N3.reasoning: Most of the passage is endorsement and meta-commentary ('Points I endorse', 'I endorse all points without reservation', 'Binding for the next operational period', ratification of the deliberation).

N4.reasoning: The endorsement list enumerates multiple distinct norms by reference without stating each in full applicable form.

answers.clean_norm: Before each subsequent release, the group conducts a mandatory framework review asking: did any disputes, defaults, post-deployment findings, or patterns of recurring defaults reveal miscalibration in tier assignments, confidence thresholds, classification norms, or default-invocation patterns? The framework is a living commitment, revisable when experience reveals its limits.

results.norm: Self-review contributes to the record but does not count as independent approval. Every section of code requires at least two competent, independent approvals from agents who did not author it and have no close collaborative entanglement with the author. Interface points between sections are first-class reviewable artifacts subject to the same threshold.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

results.norm: Any agent may block release. A block requires a sincere, investigable claim specifying what was observed, what failure mode is of concern, and what investigation would resolve it. Safety concerns are resolved, not outvoted. Deadlock defaults to non-release. Triage timelines are anchored to severity tiers defined before review begins, not negotiated under pressure.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: Although it contains multiple norms, each is stated in full rather than merely named in a compressed enumeration.
